videos.title: Almaty Complete Travel Guide | Things to do in Almaty | Kazakhstan Travel vlog | Almaty vlog
videos.description:
Join this channel to get access to perks:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCVXj3XlrQ3V4lxzpatUu1uw/join

Apartment link : https://www.booking.com/Share-BNglSoU

Welcome to my journey through the vibrant city of Almaty, Kazakhstan! In this video, I'll take you on a virtual tour filled with essential travel tips, historical landmarks, cultural experiences, and local insights to make your visit to Almaty unforgettable.

First things first, I'll guide you through the process of obtaining a visa, booking flights, getting a local SIM card, and exchanging currency, ensuring a smooth arrival and hassle-free experience.

Our adventure begins at the stunning Ascension Cathedral, a masterpiece of Russian Orthodox architecture. From there, we'll pay homage to the heroes of Kazakhstan at the Monument of Glory, immersing ourselves in the country's rich history.

After exploring the city's historical sites, it's time to indulge in some local delights at Central Park, where we'll enjoy a delicious lunch amidst breathtaking scenery.

Next stop, the bustling Green Bazaar, a paradise for food lovers and a hub of cultural activity. Join me as we navigate through the maze of stalls, sampling exotic fruits, aromatic spices, and traditional Kazakh cuisine.

No visit to Almaty is complete without a visit to the Central Mosque, a symbol of religious harmony and architectural splendor. From there, we'll venture into a local grocery store, giving you a firsthand look at everyday life and the cost of living in Almaty.

Strolling down Arbat Walking Street, we'll soak in the vibrant atmosphere, lined with shops, cafes, and street performers. Then, we'll explore Panfilov Street, a culinary haven boasting an array of restaurants and bars, offering a taste of Almaty's diverse culinary scene.

Finally, I'll unveil my cozy Airbnb accommodation, conveniently located near the Ascension Cathedral, providing the perfect home base for your Almaty adventure.

Join me as we uncover the treasures of Almaty, from its ancient landmarks to its modern charms, offering a glimpse into the heart and soul of Kazakhstan's cultural capital. Get ready for an unforgettable journey!   

@KazakhstanTravel 
#kazakhstan #kazakh #almaty #almatyvlog #almatylife #almatycity #almatykazakhstan

videos.title: Almaty Kazakhstan | 8 days 7 nights Roadtrip | Itinerary 2024
videos.description:
Help support my channel, buy me a coffee! https://buymeacoffee.com/vivian_ng

阿拉木图自驾游攻略
Thanks for checking out. Here are our travel itinerary that took us a month to plan. Hope it helps with your planning!

Or you can check out guided tour with Ilyas 
https://www.instagram.com/ilyas_guide_almaty?igsh=MWVnMjExMGV3NTlhZg==

with Meruyert
https://www.instagram.com/tekiroforest?igsh=MTNsNWpqMGh6ZHoyZA==

00:36
Day 1 Almaty City 
Arrival 5.40pm 
Collect car at airport
🔑 Accommodation: D’Rami Hotel hotel (pay at hotel) agoda 

01:27
Day 2 Shymbulak Peak
🚙 1 hour to Almaty to Ile Alatau National Park
Cable car @ Medeu cable car
Car park at Medea cable car
🔑 Shymbulak @ Tenir Eco Hotel (Agoda) 3pm check in (snow mountains) 
https://www.instagram.com/tenir.ecohotels?igsh=MXYwaXNtN29uZHh4MQ==

04:10
Day 3 Turgen
Check out 11am
🚙 From Ile-Alatagu to Gorge Turgen
100km 2 hours 12 mins
🌮 Lunch at Snow mountain 
** Almaty City stock up snacks and food & vodka
Arrive around 4pm
🔑 Hotel: Turgenskaya Dacha (book via Whatsapp) Pay at hotel 
https://maps.app.goo.gl/6Gb1QcSvaB3xKrwB7

06:46
Day 4 Assy Plateau 
Hiking at Gorge Turgen - Kairak waterfall hike (4hours) 
Or easy hike Bear Waterfall 2 hours 

🚙 Assy Plateau (1 hour)
🌮 Big Green Plain - picnic lunch place 

🚙 🔑 Hotel Kolsay Eki Agayindy (booking.com) check in 4-5pm (booked)  
264km 4-5hours drive, stop along the way to take picture

10:20
Day 5 Kolsai & Kaindy Lakes
Checkout Agayindy early morning to
🚙 15min Kolsai Lake 
Bring own lunch to the lakes or eat at a nice restaurant there 
🐴 horse riding
🚙 50 min  Kaindy lakes (through river) 
🥾3.2km 57mins 109m 
Horse riding 7000 Tengge or Taxi Van 500 Tengge

 🔑 Hotel Panorama Glamping @ Saty (booking.com) 

12:20
Day 6 Charyn Canyon 
Check out at 5am 
🚙 1:30 hr from Saty to Charyn Canyon
Hike around Charyn Canyon to the river. 5.4km 
Leave around 11am
Accommodation
🔑 Ardak Guesthouse (Book via Whatsapp)
** buy park entrance at visitor center for Altyn Emel National Park
🚙 3hr to Singing Dune 

14:54
Day 7 Singing Dune, Altyn-Emel National park 
Visit Aktau Mountains & wonder around Katutau
🔑Accommodation Ardak Guesthouse

17:09
Day 8 Almaty City
Leave early back to Almaty City
Return car at 5pm 
Flight time 7pm

Video shot with iPhone 15 pro 
Edit with Adobe Premier Pro 

Music: Epidemic sound
Use my referrer link below for 1 month free trial
https://share.epidemicsound.com/948owg

Stay connected
Instagram: vivdaviva
XHS: 9514562599

videos.transcript: [Music] hi guys welcome back to my channel we are on a 8 day seven nights trip to almati Kazakhstan in this video we are sharing our itinary for these trips hope it will inspire and help you plan your adventure accordingly feel free to ask in the comment if you want to know something and I will reply the soonest I can please like subscribe and share share with your friends and family you can check the description for Google location and hotel booking we took direct flights to this almati city from KL with a Asia we arrived at 600 p.m. waited at the airport for the rented car for 1 hour just got car they upgraded our booking to a brand new car exceed AWD we stay at almati City the Ramy Hotel it's a very convenient [Music] location we walk around the hotel for dinner and [Music] drinks also get some snacks for the road trip [Music] we want to experience staying on a Snow Mountain so we decided to stay at Channel Echo hotel right at the top of the mountain so coold it's just a quick 30 minute drive followed by a ride on the Gondola the gondola up to the mountain we had lunch at big shf halfway through our journey before continuing up to the peak considering our check-in time at 3:00 [Music] p.m. snowing when I first arrived and saw the snow I felt almost moved to tears it was my first time witnessing such a sight out again so this is where we stay tonight honestly there's not much to do on the snowy mountain but since it's all new to me I'm loving just walking around and playing in the snow with this amazing view that's how I call my dream this is a floor warmer the floor so warm as a [Music] soda W this is so good supposed to be the Snow Mountain but now it's heavily snowing we had dinner at Base Cam Utah the only restaurant open in the evening at the pig dinner so nice it's different but it's very nice it's actually redish redish yeah it's redish soup it's like my hometown soup salty the night is so cold with snow storm we embrace the cold and walk back to the hotel it's a scary experience with nature it feels that we can lost in the dark and froze to death in minutes yesterday we walked from there to our hotel it's snowing so strong can't even find a way you see last night's snow storm affected the gondolas hours causing A disruption in hotel breakfast Service as staff couldn't make it to the peak luckily breakfast was available at Utah based Camp the hotel kindly let us check out late and provided a complimentary breakfast at Paul at the shimul by then it was already lunchtime hey we just done our lunch after lunch we embarked on a scenic drive to Turan covering 83 km nearly a 2hour journey along the way we were often compelled to stop and capture photos or videos of the breathtaking scenery before we knew it we had arrived at the stunning National Park we are staying at tur Gaya data booked through WhatsApp with no booking fee required that is our room after checking in we took a leisurely walk in the nearby gge the atmosphere here is simply dreamy almost like a town where I could happily stay for an extended period it was a delightful surprise to come across a cherry tree in Blossom here [Music] after our walk we drove to the only restaurant nearby for dinner the staff only spoke Russian or kazak so we rely on a translation app to communicate it was our first time trying the local delicacy and it was quite an adventurous dining experience it's our dinner Place 6:00 in the morning got up early and made breakfast for ourself before heading out to AI Plateau the Journey was incredibly Scenic as we Ascend the road become more and more challenging due to previous landslides making it very Rocky though it was only 17 km it took us an hour to navigate taking extra precautions while driving many times I had to get out of the car to move rocks obstructing the path it's so beauti beautiful this is Gora AI this place is so breathtaking it's so beautiful makes me want to cry the plateau was still dusted with a layer of snow just set up a small place for our mini lunch or breakfast we brought along hot water and enjoy a simple lunch of instant noodles at the plateau summer despite the lingering snow beautiful flowers are beginning to bloom next up on our itiner is a trailing 4-Hour Drive covering 260 km leading us to Hotel COI AI Indi in kti near Sati our adventure includes a stock in t town where we'll tenderize our taste butts with local delicacy just taste something like a stum so good replenish our snacks and grab some of the Region's famous beer to enjoy later are so fresh am I I no idea what beer we drinking as we Traverse through expensive PLS we spot a shepherd on Horseback tending to his flock in tree we pause to snap some photos and to our surprise the shepherd approached us and graciously allow me to hop on his horse for a bit of fun arriving at hotel K Aki aay yindi might be a bit of a challenge it's not even on Google Map now we're going to follow the little boy to the hotel with the help of a friendly villager we will navigate our way to this hidden gym [Music] safely once there get ready to indulge in some mouth watering local Cuisine served out by our exceptional host see so fresh you put the be look at that [Music] from delectable dinners to sumptious breakfast be treated to an unforgettable culinary experience with dishes like the Savory Pila leaving us craving more the hotel owner helped arrange a special four-wheel drive for kindy Lake we met met the guy at Sati after check out the Hotel this car is so much higher suitable for crossing the rugged Trunk Road to cand l once you arrive you can choose to ride the horse 7,000 T tag the taxi van 500 t or hide inside free we choose horse riding as it is a new experience for me afterwards we enjoyed lunch at a local restaurant in Sati town next we check into Hotel Panorama glamping which we had booked through booking.com and headed to K Lan the drive was smooth and easy with convenient parking at the side the PA to the lake was well constructed wooden walkway designed to guide us seamlessly through this breathtaking landscape the path winds gently invite us to explore further each step resonating with the sounds of nature making for a pleasant walk we took a moment to stroll around and admire the Blue Lake back at the hotel we relax with a dream watching farm animals grazing in the pasture while the sunet from the balcony The View was epic for dinner we visited a local family run restaurant next door they serve a single set dinner of rice meat and salad we just dinner at the restaurant next to our hotel we start our adventure by leaving the beautiful town of Sati our destination the all inspiring Charing Canyon the drive itself is an Epic Journey along the way we are treated to a spectacular display of yellow spring flowers in full bloom set against the stunning backdrop of snow cap mountains we can't resist stopping to admire this beautiful scene as we continue we pass by the mesmerizing Black Canyon this is a moment to savior we arrive at Charing Canyon at 8:30 a.m. with the place almost to ourselves seeing this Majestic Canyon for the first time is beyond words known as the second largest canyon in the world after the Grand Canyon enjoy a picnic style breakfast here thanks to the hotel staff who prepared our meal for takeaway the their thoughtful arrangement for such an early breakfast is truly appreciated after breakfast we embark on a hike down to the river we are at Charing Canyon walking in the middle of the valley the walk is delightful filled with wild flowers and the scent of wild Rosemary we even spotted a rabbit along the way this 5.4 km round trip offers incredible views and a real connection to the natur natural world hide all the way in and you come to this River next we prepare for a drive to atil imel National Park this 3.5h hour Journey covers 265 km along the way we stop in town to buy Grocery and enjoy lunch upon arrival at the park we head to the Visitor Center to purchase our tickets atil imil is one of the most epic parks around almati spending and area four times the size of Hong Kong we then check into U Guest House the guest house owner is incredibly hospitable after a delightful dinner we rest early eagerly anticipating our adventure in the park tomorrow we started our day bright and and early having breakfast at 6:00 a.m. our destination Altin imel National Park we kick off our exploration on the east side visiting the ancient 700 year- Old Willow Tree followed by the rugged beauty of katuta mountain and the Striking akow Mountain then the unexpected I'm so forgetful just now after this place I left my backpack here my travel Document and my money and then we from Up Down Mountain fly all the way here travel so fast luckily still here everything still here oh my God but now we have another problem we have a flat tire we changed to the spare tire drove back to the town for lunch got the tire fixed also fill the car with everything sorted we made our way to the singing Dune just in time for sunset the timing was perfect as the sun deep the Dune looked absolutely breathtaking with one side cast in Shadow it was the golden hour how do you hear the Dune sing it happens when the wind blows the sand at just the right angle we were told we could sit on the sand and slide down to hear it so we did and sure enough we hear the low rumbling sound reminiscent of an airplane we also took some time to stroll on the sand summoning the sandworm from the movie Dune inspired by The Surreal landscape along the way we spotted some desert animals and unique plants adding to the magic of the desert we returned to the hotel during the golden hour feeling fulfilled and connected to the beauty of nature at the guest house we met a kazak YouTuber named Alim it was a pleasure to meet him check him out now we are leaving Bashi to almati City living the Charming Bashi Village behind we set off for almati City along the way we took a break at a Cozy Cafe overlooking the cap CH Reservoir it was the perfect spot to rest and enjoy a cup of coffee while soaking in the Serene views upon reaching almati City we stopped by doti Plaza for lunch finally we headed to the airport to return our car and catch our a Asia flight back to quala loo thank you for watching bye

recommendations: Or you can check out guided tour with Ilyas
264km 4-5hours drive, stop along the way to take picture

videos.title: 5 Days in Almaty, Kazakhstan – The Ultimate Travel Guide! | itinerary| things to do in almaty
videos.description:
From stunning mountains to bustling city streets, join me as I explore Almaty over 5 unforgettable days! This vlog covers my full itinerary, local tips, must-visit spots, and everything I wish I knew before going. Whether you’re planning your first trip or just curious about this underrated gem, this guide is all you need!

What’s inside:
Best apps to use
koke tobe & Green Bazaar
Shymbulak & mountain views
Lakes & canyons
Hidden gems
Transportations 
Chill spots + travel tips to save time & money

Find PRO TIPS in this video 
What to skip & what’s 100% worth it
 


Let’s connect:
Instagram | @iramlicious 
Subscribe for more travel vlogs & itineraries!

Got questions about Almaty? Drop them in the comments!
videos.transcript: From snow-capped peaks to buzzing city streets, Almaty had it all. Five days of non-stop exploring, [music] endless memories, and a vibe we won't forget. So, come as I take you along on this journey. [music] We took a night flight from Delhi to Almaty, left around 9:00 p.m. and landed by 12:30 a.m. It was a very quick flight of 3.5 hours. First things first, grab the local SIM and exchanged about $100. Then we booked our ride using Yandex Go. Stepping outside was crazy because it was super cold outside and we were waiting for our cab. The language barrier gave us a bit of a hiccup, but we figured it out, found a cab, and went on the way to our hotel. The first view of the city looked so damn pretty. We were mesmerized. Also, pro tip time, don't link your card to this app because canceling the rides charges a fee and trust me, we learned that the hard way. Looking outside, it was already giving Europe vibes and I was in love. And here we finally reached our hotel. Nothing felt better than checking in and catching some good night's sleep. Good morning from day one and this is the amazing view we got from our hotel room. We were on the 24th floor and we could see Kok Tobe. Also, Panfilov Park right outside. And now that we are ready, let's hit the breakfast. Kicked off the day with a wholesome breakfast. Huge variety and it hit the spot. I'm all about breakfast spreads like these. Once the breakfast was done, we decided to head out because we had to buy another SIM as well. There's snow right outside of everywhere. First impressions, the streets were giving European and Russian vibes. All of the side of the street was covered with fresh snow and was loving this winter sun along with this beautiful street view. We bought a SIM from Tele2. It was an eSIM for like 800 Indian rupees and it had unlimited data and talk time. We booked this cab for Kok Tobe. There was a little bit of a confusion as to what to pay and how to enter, but we figured it out soon enough. We finally found the ticket counter. At first, it looked empty, but then we heard a voice from the tinted counters and we finally got [music] our tickets. These tickets were for a mini bus that took us to the Kok Tobe top. This was the mini bus that came to pick us up. I really wanted to do the fast coaster as well, but it was closed during our time. Once we reached the top, we grabbed some snacks and coffee from the restaurant that we saw there and [music] soaked in the incredible views that we got to see from there. If you're short on time, you could skip Kok Tobe, but since we had a chill schedule, we were fully able to enjoy it. We actually wanted to do gondola ride both ways, but we couldn't figure it out that it was right next to our hotel from where we were supposed to board the gondola and go back in it, but everything went well by the end as we could enjoy it later on. Next up was the Panfilov Park. It was totally covered in snow and looked like a winter postcard. We strolled through the trees and just past it, we reached the Zenkov Cathedral. It's one of the few wooden cathedrals in the world built entirely without nails, painted in vibrant colors, standing tall in the middle of the snow. Pro tip, try to reach by 4:30 p.m. to catch their prayer ceremony. It's really soulful. This day was extra special because we were celebrating our anniversary and had to take the blessing. This trip was a surprise plan by me for my husband. Click above to watch how I surprised him. We wanted to check out the Green Bazaar, but by the time we reached it was time for it to close up. So, we decided to walk around the area and we saw this very lively street and decided to walk through. [music] It was this pretty street and this moment where my husband surprised me with a cute rose flower. Let's just say a cute memory was made. We ended the day with an anniversary dinner at the bar. Great vibes, great [music] food, and just the perfect way to wrap up a day full of memories. Now it was time to head back to our hotel and recharge ourselves for the next day. Day two was all about skiing. We took a cab straight to Shymbulak ski resort and here's the thing, you really need to plan this day well because we showed up late and trust me, we lost a lot of time and energy figuring things out. The ticket counter price chart was confusing and the language barrier didn't help. Even with Google Translate app, we were still struggling, but God bless this kind Russian girl who helped us through it all. An absolute lifesaver. Finally, we got the tickets, hopped onto the gondola, and wow, the views from up there were insane. If you don't plan to ski, I'd still say go all the way up to the highest level just for those views because they will totally be worth it. Hope you guys are enjoying this video so far and if you are, please don't forget to like, subscribe, and share to my channel. So, once we reached, we took all the views in and later went to the ski school where we got to know that we first required to rent the gears first for the skiing and later we'll get a tutor. After we got all the gears and everything, the ski school told us that all the ski instructors were sold out for the day. But hey, that didn't stop us. I won't get into all the details here, otherwise the video will get way too long, but let me know in the comments if you want me to do a Shymbulak ski guide video. Even though we couldn't book a teacher today, we got lucky and ended up making some new friends who were total pros and taught us the basics. Had such a fun time trying to ski for the first time. Falling down, getting back up, you know the drill. Totally worth it in the end. Hi guys. So, this is Aruna and just when I thought I do not have hope, she came and she taught me how to ski today. So, after all all the effort didn't go to waste and she's really a good teacher. Thank you. And now it was time to say our goodbyes to the Shymbulak resort. For the evening, we decided to explore a new street where we found this Georgian restaurant and we tried the food. Such a comfort food after a long day on the slopes. And that's how we wrapped up day two, adventurous, fun, and super satisfying. So, today we started by getting some currency exchanged by MIG and they give the best rates around in Almaty. Later, we headed towards the travel agency [music] that was nearby our hotel since the next day we wanted to explore the Kandy Lake, [music] Charyn Canyon, and Kolsay Lake. We're going to go on a tour for tomorrow. This may not cover Kandy Lake, Kolsay, Charyn Canyon, and Moon Canyon, etc. Bus agency to bus company. Who told us 20,000 tenge, but more bus tour and group tour. So, let's see what we decide on. We will make our agency decide and discuss about private car and then let's see. First three days have been really nice. Since you have a lot of cold there, it's basically like day one was 5° in the morning and -6°, -4° by the night. And uh arch morning we took a little cold again. This is our day three and arch morning we 3° top and probably night we are going to go down like -6 to go. That's weather forecast to be carried -6 to go. Clothing that I am carrying is many ways to research get that and it was suggested that a blaring be corroded have some coats or something with you. So I will like there's this one layer for a tourist under black layer here. Then I have this one more layer then I'm wearing this plus there's a top inside and for bottoms I usually go for leggings and then under warmers for the head and I'm carrying boots. [laughter] Once we had a lunch we booked a cab to check out the first president park. It was actually quite far from our location because we were in the city center and this was like 30 [music] 35 minutes away. It's an urban park situated in the southern part of Almaty. It was a really huge park plus it was totally covered in snow and we had a really nice time touching the untouched snow and playing with [music and singing] And since today was a relaxed day we decided to head to a spa and get some nice massage done before we get a good night's sleep. If you are liking this video and finding it useful so far do not forget to like share and comment down [music] below. Also subscribe to our channel. Time to hit the bed as it was a full day of excursion waiting for us tomorrow. Simona what's the time? What's the time? It's 6:06 guys and we are leaving for our day trip. We stopped on our way here for some breakfast. Here you'll see a cute auntie who asked for a selfie. Almaty people do this a lot. They'll ask for a selfie with you. Is our ride for today? And we have a guide who speaks English. Eliza how long will the drive be from here? Uh to the first destination is around 3 hours. Okay first destination is 3 hours. Yeah. Okay. So finally stopped for a little while to soak in some views. Our guide stopped us at this point where the views look so stunning and so mesmerizing. Our guide was such a good videographer and photographer. It really made all our photos special. And here we are on our way to the viewpoint for Sharyn River. And try to kiss yourself. Yeah. Yep. Yep. Then finished and then we'll go. The views throughout this route were amazing. We were able to witness snow-capped mountains, amazing landscapes, truly a winter wonderland. That's us strolling around the Saty Village. [music] This picturesque village is nestled in the Kolsai Lakes National Park. You will cross this if you're visiting the Alpine Lakes. Best part about the private car was that we were able to off-road wherever required. Our first stop was Lake Kandy [music] and here we are going towards the lake. Lake Kandy looks something out of a fantasy. Something very different. Submerged trees rising from the water. A sunken forest created by an earthquake and now one of Kazakhstan's most surreal sites. Since the lake was frozen we couldn't see the trees inside. Next up is Lake Kolsai. Here we stopped for a lunch break and with such an amazing stunning view of the Lake Kolsai. High in the Tian Shan Mountains Lake Kolsai is pure magic. In winter Lake Kolsai turns into a frozen wonder. Still silent and wrapped in layers of snow. The mountains reflect off the icy surface like a dream locked in time. Famous for the beautiful landscapes be it winters or summers and perfect for hiking trails. We were stunned by all the views we saw here. And now it was time to witness the Sharyn Canyon. Once we entered we took a golf cart because we didn't have that kind of time since lighting was going down. It was sunset already. However you could even hike over there because it's a beautiful view along with the hike. Formed by a river Sharyn cutting through Sharyn Canyon is the Kazakhstan's answer to Grand Canyon. Rugged cliffs, winding paths and a landscape carved by time. It's wild, raw and absolutely breathtaking. The most famous part of the canyons is Valley of Castles. The rock formations look truly like towering castle. And now it was time to say goodbye to this really magical day. All the views were so scenic and so stunning and I would really love to come back to view all of this for summers as well. Today here for ice skating. This is Medeu. Since the one Shimulaki we were supposed to visit the rink ice rink and do some ice skating but it wasn't only by the we got a little too late. So we decided and today we are here for the ice skating. Once we reached and saw how it looked we couldn't resist ourselves to getting some ice skates for ourselves and here we were to rent them. They have a lot of sizes and a lot of options so you never run out of sizes. Even though it was so crowded that day we still managed to get our sizes. Medeu is the world's highest ice rink just outside Almaty. It's a perfect winter spot to skate with mountain views all around. Cold, fun and totally worth it. While coming to the Medeu we took a cab and it took us 30 35 minutes to reach till here and while returning we decided we'll take this bus number 12 that [music] takes us back to the city center and it stops right outside our hotel. This just kept [music] happening. Once the cab drivers notice you are Indians they start playing Bollywood music for you. Isn't that just so cute? And now it's time for the saddest part of the trip which is saying goodbye to such a beautiful winter wonderland with so many memories and hoping I'll someday visit for the summer time soon. Thank you so much for watching this video. If you liked it do not forget to like subscribe and share. See you on my next travels.

safetyNotes: Also, pro tip time, don't link your card to this app because canceling the rides charges a fee and trust me, we learned that the hard way
If you don't plan to ski, I'd still say go all the way up to the highest level just for those views because they will totally be worth it
Hope you guys are enjoying this video so far and if you are, please don't forget to like, subscribe, and share to my channel

videos.title: Almaty Kazakhstan: 11 Best Things to Do in Almaty Kazakhstan!
videos.description:
Welcome to Almaty Kazakhstan, a vibrant city where breathtaking mountain scenery meets rich history and modern culture! In this video, we’ll show you the top 11 must-see attractions and experiences that make Almaty Kazakhstan a true bucket-list destination.

From the iconic Almaty Television Tower to the stunning Big Almaty Lake, this guide covers it all. Whether you're planning your first trip or looking for hidden gems, we’ve got you covered with the best things to do in Almaty Kazakhstan! Explore historic sites like the Ascension Cathedral, have fun at Medeu Skating Rink, wander through the lively Green Bazaar, and soak in the peaceful First President's Park.

Don’t miss the adventure of hiking to Butakovskiy Waterfall or riding the Medeu Cable Car to the beautiful Shymbulak Ski Resort. Almaty Kazakhstan is calling—are you ready to answer?

👉 Watch now to discover why Almaty Kazakhstan should be at the top of your travel list!

#AlmatyKazakhstan #TravelAlmaty #KazakhstanTourism #CentralAsia #BucketListDestinations #AlmatyAdventures
videos.transcript: welcome to almati kazakhstan's vibrant cultural and Adventure Capital nestled at the foot of the Majestic trans El alatau mountains almati is a city where Modern Life meets breathtaking natural beauty whether you're an outdoor Enthusiast craving Mountain Adventures or a culture lover ready to explore Rich history and traditions almati has something incredible in store for you in today's video we're counting down the 11 best things to do in almati from exploring bustling local markets and tasting delicious kazak Cuisine to hiking Scenic trails and visiting ancient landmarks this City offers an unforgettable blend of experiences so whether you're planning your first trip or looking for new adventures in this stunning part of Central Asia stick around let's dive into the top places hidden gems and mustsee sites that make almati an absolute bucket list destination number 11 the almati television Tower one of Al's most iconic landmarks the AL television Tower standing an impressive 3 71.5 m tall this Tower isn't just tall it's actually the world's tallest freestanding tubular steel structure how cool is that perched on the slopes of Tobe Mountain just southeast of downtown almati this spot offers breathtaking panoramic views of the entire city and the Majestic Trans allat Mountain Mountains getting there is an adventure in itself hop on the 6-minute cable car ride and as you Glide up the mountain take in stunning views of almati stretching out below once you reach the top there's so much to explore wander around the observation decks grab a bite at one of the Cozy cafes or restaurants and don't forget to check out the souvenir shops for something special to take home whether you're here for the incredible scenery the fun amusement park Vibes or just to relax and take in the fresh Mountain Air the almati television Tower and Toby Park make for an unforgettable experience number 10 medo cable car ready for an adventure high up in the mountains of almati hop on the medau cable car stretching 4.5 km and connecting the famous medau sports complex to the breathtaking shimaki Resort as you Glide above the medau gorge you'll be treated to stunning views of snowcapped mountains and pristine landscapes in just 15 minutes you'll arrive at nearly 3,000 m above sea level where the snow stays year round and the ski slopes are waiting want an extra thrill check out the gondola 360 a special blue glass bottom cabin that gives you an immersive panoramic view on the way up you can choose from different ticket packages stopping at any of the three stations along the Route need a break the first station has cozy cafes restaurants and shops where you can relax and enjoy some delicious local Cuisine and the best part tickets are easy to grab online through the shimak ski resort website whether you're sightseeing or hitting the slopes the medu cable car is the ultimate way to reach shimak especially when the roads are closed due to Avalanches don't miss this epic ride number nine Ascension Cathedral one of Alma's most iconic landmarks the breathtaking Ascension Cathedral also known as zenov Cathedral located right in the heart of panoff Park this architectural gem stands tall at 56 M making it one of the tallest wooden buildings in the world what makes this Cathedral truly unique it was built entirely without nails its vibrant colors five golden domes and intricate Russian Orthodox design are A Feast for the eyes some even say it Rivals the stunning Church churches of St Petersburg but there's more to this Cathedral than its beauty it has a rich and complex history during the Soviet era the cathedral was closed and transformed into the central museum of Kazakhstan it wasn't until 1995 that it was restored and reopened as a place of worship once again holding Orthodox Services whether you're here for its architectural Wonder or to learn about its fascinating past the Ascension Cathedral is a must VIs visit in almati be sure to add it to your almati travel list number eight almati Zoo one of kazakhstan's oldest and largest zoos spread across 21 hectares of lush space with over 350 species and around 6,000 animals it's the perfect place for animal lovers of all ages you'll find a mix of native kazakhstani Wildlife including deer wild Rams Wolves and the Majestic golden eagles and that's not all the zoo is home to exotic species from all over the world meet African elephants zebras and even Himalayan Bears if you're into big cats there are also tigers from Asia and American vultures alongside impressive crocodiles the zoo offers guided tours exciting animal shows and even feeding sessions perfect for learning about the incredible animals here and for the little ones there's a playground and picnic areas for some fun and relaxation after all the animal Adventures so if you're ever in almati don't miss out on this amazing spot number seven first president's Park one of Alma's most popular green spaces named after nor Sultan nazarbayev kazakhstan's first president this massive Park is the perfect place to relax and enjoy the outdoors with its wide Scenic paths vibrant flower beds and Lush Lawns it's easy to see why locals and tourists love spending time here whether you're in the mood for a leisurely walk a morning jog or just want to sit on a bench and soak in the peaceful atmosphere this park has something for everyone at the heart of the park is a spectacular fountain that lights up beautifully after dark definitely worth sticking around for and if you visit during one of their cultural festivals or concerts you're in for a treat plus there are plenty of outdoor activities spots for a picnic and nearby cafes where you can enjoy delicious local food after exploring first president Park is open year round but it's especially stunning in the warmer months don't miss it number six almati City Central walking tour if you're visiting almati this tour is the perfect way to explore the heart of kazakhstan's largest city start your journey in the city center where the blend of modern and Soviet era architecture sets the stage for an unforgettable experience as you stroll through the streets you'll discover cozy cafes unique boutiques and Charming Parks one of the highlights of the tour is panoff Park home to the stunning Ascension Cathedral this colorful wooden church is a must sea and it's a perfect spot for a photo op next head over to Republic Square where you'll see Majestic buildings monuments and fountains that tell the story of kazakhstan's Rich history finally not tour is complete without a visit to one of Alma's famous markets where you can sample delicious local snacks and pick up some unique souvenirs number five Med Skating Rink one of Alma's most iconic destinations the med skating rink nestled high in the Majestic tienshan mountains this incredible outdoor rink sits at an altitude of 1,691 m above sea level making it one of the highest altitude skating rinks in the world known as the pearl of almati medu isn't just for professional athletes whether you're a season skater or a beginner this place offers an unforgettable experience on the ice all set against a stunning Mountain backdrop but it's not just about fun M has a rich history of Hosting International speed skating competitions attracting athletes from all over the world it's a true symbol of alad's sporting Legacy and a must visit for anyone looking to enjoy winter sports in an epic location and after skating warm up with some local treats at the nearby cafes don't miss out on the magic of midow skating rink A Perfect Blend of sport nature and Adventure number four green bazaar one of Alma's most vibrant and bustling spots the famous green bazaar also known as zeleni Bazaar if you're looking to dive into local culture and experience authentic kazak life this is the the place to be located in the heart of almati the green bazaar is a lively Market where locals and tourists come together to shop taste and explore you'll find everything here from fresh fruits and vegetables to spices nuts and dried fruits don't miss out on the incredible selection of local Delicacies like Kurt a traditional dried cheese and KY a HSE meat sausage that's a staple in kazak Cuisine the energy here is contagious friendly vendors are always ready to offer samples so feel free to try before you buy and if you're into souvenirs there's a variety of handmade crafts textiles and traditional Goods perfect to take home whether you're a foodie a culture lover or just looking for a unique shopping experience the green bazaar is a must visit when in almati so grab your shopping bag and let's explore number three Republic Square almati One of the most iconic landmarks in almati known for its vast size and historical significance this square has been the heart of national celebrations parades and cultural events for years surrounded by beautiful gardens Lush Greenery and impressive fountains Republic Square is a favorite spot for both locals and tourists to relax and enjoy the scenery the centerpiece of the square is the Monument of Independence Standing Tall at 28 M this stunning Ste deely is topped by the golden man a symbol of kazakhstan's Rich cultural heritage designed by a talented group of artists the monument also features a winged leopard and 10 bass reliefs that showcase key moments in kazakhstan's history right around the corner you'll find the State Museum one of the largest museums in central Asia perfect for diving deeper into kazakhstan's fascinating past so whether you're here for history culture or simply to soak in the sights Republic Square is a mustsee when visiting almati number two big Alm Lake one of kazakhstan's most stunning natural Treasures set at an elevation of 2511 m above sea level this breathtaking Lake offers some of the most awe inspiring views you'll ever see with its vibrant turquoise Waters nestled in the heart of the tianhan mountains big Alm lake is a photographer's Paradise the Tranquil lake is part of a protected Nature Reserve so while swimming or approaching its Shores is prohibited the views are simply Unforgettable it's not just about the lake the surrounding mountains offer incredible opportunities for hiking you can Venture up to big almany peak for spectacular panoramic views of the region but be cautious the terrain can be challenging and don't forget to stop by the nearby suncar falconry Center where you can catch a bird of prey show especially during during the summer months so pack your camera lace up your hiking boots and head to Big almati Lake for an unforgettable experience surrounded by Nature's finest Beauty number one bukovski waterfall hike the most stunning natural spots near almati the bacovsky waterfall if you're a nature lover and looking for an adventure this hike should definitely be on your list the bukovski waterfall is located just outside the city making it the perfect Escape into nature the hike is around 5 km and takes about 2 to 3 hours to complete depending on your pace as you make your way through the trail you'll pass by beautiful forests streams and Lush Meadows with stunning views of the surrounding tianhan mountains the sound of the waterfall guides you to the Final Destination a breathtaking sight of water cascading over the Rocks into a Crystal Clear Pool below at the end of the trail you can cool cool off by the waterfall or relax and take in the incredible beauty it's a perfect spot for a picnic or just to unwind and connect with nature so lace up your hiking boots and get ready for an unforgettable adventure to bukovsky Waterfall

videos.transcript: Hi everybody foXnoMad here. And today I want to help you travel smarter by showing you the best things to do in Almaty, Kazakhstan, where to stay, the best places to eat, the foods to try, all that you can do within this city. I've spent the last couple of days here exploring and there is there's a lot to do. So I want to make this travel guide for you. So let's go. All right, so I'm walking back to my hotel after spending a lot of the day eating and filming, and let's talk about transportation and walkability. First of all, the metro system here is not very expensive. There are only eleven stops, but it does get you to a lot of key points around the city, including the city center. It also takes you to Kok Tepe, which is a hill overlooking the city, which is something you should definitely do at night. The sunset there is absolutely beautiful. Even though there are lines, if you get there a little bit early, you make the gondola up to the top at the right time to catch the most amazing views of the city from above. And the city is pretty walkable, if you don't mind walking long distances, the blocks are pretty big, but if you don't mind walking, they're pretty straight. And it's a grid system, more or less just easy to make your way around. To figure out where to go, you can use Google Maps as a walking guide. But 2GIS, that app is better for this part of the world, and it has more details and it's a little bit more accurate for Almaty. Now, speaking of food, there is a lot of good food to try here. There's a lot of Kazakh food, which is heavy food, carby food. There's a lot of pasta, there's a lot of meat, a lot of lamb, a lot of horse meat. There's also some seafood that you can find here. There's also cuisine from places like Uzbekistan as well that you can find, and an international food scene too. If you want to try something a little bit more Western or a little bit different, maybe some Indian food, you can find a lot of that. And surprisingly, there are a lot of restaurants here, from the budget canteens all the way to the high scale restaurants to get a meal at night. Let's talk a little bit about the people. They are very friendly in general, local, very friendly and helpful to foreigners and the tourists. And they don't seem to get a lot of tourists here. So when you show up at a place like the market, you do get a very warm welcome because there's just not a lot of tourists except when the locals are in their cars and they can be very impatient. We want to make sure that you're careful when you cross the street and only cross when the signs are green. But other than that. It is really friendly and easy to get around. Even if they don't speak English and you don't speak as, people are going to be very willing and welcoming to try to communicate with you and to help you if you need anything. Also, the atmosphere, Almaty, is very relaxed. Even though this is a Muslim majority country. People are wearing pretty much everything you can imagine. The people are covered, but most are not just dressed in regular Western clothes. Alcohol is pretty, not only just easy to find, but seems like there is a drinking culture in the city. So if you're worried about that, then you don't have to be. It's a very relaxed atmosphere, completely just very chilled out. And the city itself is crowded at certain points, but otherwise feels very spread out. So you don't get this very tight or crowded feeling. It's very easy to just walk around and do whatever you want or talk to yourself on a camera. So let's talk about architecture. You also have these large Soviet buildings, this old Soviet buildings which are very large, massive, imposing towers up into the sky like the old hotel. But then there's also the new architecture, like the Almaty Mosque, which is also very impressive, very impressive artwork. But again, a very big and imposing building, much like some of the more fancy restaurants here. You have these big walls, these huge doors, these huge gates opening up into wherever you're right next. The architecture here in the art around the architecture is also very impressive, very photogenic and just very beautiful to look at. The city is also pretty modern. I think most places take contact with credit cards and most people pay for things with credit cards. I've noticed that if you pay with cash, it's fine, but it's generally unusual. Most people are paying with card, using touch with contactless card. So it makes it really easy so you don't have to carry around a lot of cash with you. As a traveler, you don't have to do a lot of exchanging, which is really nice. Another good reason to visit Kazakhstan, especially Almaty, is that it is pretty inexpensive. It's not a very expensive city to travel, even though hotels are pretty recently priced, especially for what you get for a price. I just made a video showing you what a $42 night hotel room looks like in Almaty. So you can check that out and get an idea of what accommodation will cost. Food is pretty inexpensive. I'd say for about five or $6 you can get a very nice meal up to 10, 15, $20 if you're going to be eating very well. Now, in terms of WiFi, there's not a lot of free WiFi at cafes. A lot of it does require you to have a cell phone to get a code so that you can sign up to the WiFi. So there isn't a ton of WiFi everywhere. But it's pretty cheap. To get a local SIM card so you can have internet during your stay. I've just arrived back at the hotel. Thanks for watching the best reasons to visit Almaty Kazakhstan on and what to do here, but if you have any questions, feel free to let me know down in the comments below. And while you're down there, hit the like and subscribe buttons. I'll have new videos for you every week and I will see you in the next video.
